text: In Washington D.C., visitors were given a special present. The National Christmas Tree is reopened to the public. Its brief closure this week was linked to the partial government shutdown. President Trump and congressional Democrats remain at odds over funding for the border wall. President Trump, if you want to open the government, you must abandon the wall. Different sorts of construction concerns in California, where victims of massive wildfires were homeless this Christmas. I never wanted after the fire was, you know, my God, what's gonna happen to my town? How's it gonna rebuild? People gonna want to just leave. And I thought immediately we need to stay motivated. In the South, authorities dealing with the aftermath of two mall shootings, one in Louisiana. We didn't necessarily know what happened until we heard children and, you know, parents and everyone screaming and running through the food court. The other in Florida, where a police canine was killed. And across the country, many Americans are hoping to win the Mega Millions lottery Christmas night. The jackpot is worth three hundred twenty-one million dollars. I'm John Lawrence reporting.
